title: Micropholis crassipedicellata
taxonomy: Micropholis crassipedicellata is a species within the family Sapotaceae, which includes a diverse range of tropical fruit-bearing trees. It belongs to the order Ericales and is a part of the Sapotaceae family, which comprises approximately 200 species. As a member of the Sapotaceae family, Micropholis crassipedicellata is closely related to other sapote species. The genus Micropholis is a monotypic genus, containing only this particular species. Further classification includes its position within the subfamily Chrysophoroideae.
uses: Micropholis crassipedicellata has been traditionally used in some cultures for its medicinal properties. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can be beneficial in treating various health conditions. However, more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human use. In some areas, it is used to treat skin irritations and wounds, as well as to alleviate symptoms of fever and respiratory issues. Further studies are necessary to fully explore its potential as a medicinal plant.

intro: Micropholis crassipedicellata, also known as the 'Brazilian walnut tree', is a species of plant in the family Sapotaceae that is native to the Amazon rainforest. The bark of this plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, providing several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce fever, relieve pain, and protect against infections. The bioactive constituents of Micropholis crassipedicellata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and tannins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include decoctions and infusions, which are used to treat various health conditions, such as fever, arthritis, and skin infections.
